summary: Manish Infratech billing/invoice management web app. Clean rebuild from version 5 base. Authentication: simple username/PIN login (MANISHINFRATECH / 1752), session in localStorage; bills stored in stable canister storage (persists across logouts/upgrades). Features: Add/Edit bills with party name (autocomplete from saved names), unique invoice number, auto-populated alterable bill date, up to 15 line item rows (Sr. No., HSN Code, Product Name, Qty, Unit [free-text only], Rate, computed Amount); live GST summary (CGST 9%, SGST 9%, round-off, final amount, amount paid, pending); amount paid & pending per bill; no duplicate invoice numbers. Invoice print: red header band, white body, MI logo (red/white) top-left, MANISH INFRATECH in red, 'TAX INVOICE' label, party GST No. in header, line items table, GST summary, amount in words (print/share only), 'Thank you for your business' footer. Search by company name or bill number shows all bills of that company. Party Summary page: all companies with totals, GST No. (editable), date filter, View Report. Company Report page: per-party summary cards, date filter, bill table with Print per row, CSV download. Summary/P&L page: overall financials, Profit & Loss section, date filter, CSV download (authenticated only). Data stored in stable backend storage with correct nanosecond↔millisecond date conversion.
architectural_notes: Single-user access control: protect all routes behind authentication
Bills stored with fields: party, invoice, amount, cgst, sgst, gst, roundoff, final
Invoice numbers must be unique across all bills; enforce uniqueness check on bill creation and edit
Bills must store a date field (bill/invoice date) to support date-range filtering in reports
Party GST numbers stored in a separate party/company profile or alongside the bill; must appear in invoice print and party summary
Bill line items must store: srNo, hsnCode, productName, quantity (Float), unit (Text, e.g. SQM/RMT/PCS), rate (Float), amount (computed as quantity × rate), gst (18% of amount)
Party name and product name inputs must support autocomplete suggestions based on previously used values
Authentication uses simple username/password: username = MANISHINFRATECH, PIN = 1752 (replaces Internet Identity)
Line items support up to 15 rows per bill (revised from 30)
Unit field in line items must be a plain free-text input with no dropdown or autocomplete suggestions
known_issues: Authentication bug: app shows 'Access Denied' message but still grants access instead of blocking unauthorized users
Blank page shown on Billing and Summary pages — app not rendering after version 8 deploy
Actor not available error on bill add — bills cannot be saved; version 10 deploy broke canister actor connection
Old bills appear deleted/missing after version 10 deploy — possible data loss or canister reinstall issue
Date shows 1970 on bills — likely caused by incorrect Unix timestamp unit (nanoseconds being passed where milliseconds expected, or vice versa) in date display logic
App text appears in pure white on white/light backgrounds — text is invisible on Add Bill, Summary, and other pages; must be changed to black

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity authentication so the app is only accessible by the authenticated owner. On first login, register the caller's principal as the sole authorized owner. On all subsequent visits, gate access behind that stored principal — any other principal must be rejected and shown an 'Unauthorized' message.

Build an 'Add Bill' form with fields for Party Name, Invoice Number, and Base Amount. On submission, automatically compute CGST (9%), SGST (9%), total GST, round-off (difference between rounded integer and raw total), and final amount. Display the computed breakdown to the user before saving, then save the bill via the backend.

Build a search interface where the owner can type a keyword and retrieve all bills whose party name or invoice number contains that keyword. Display results showing Party Name, Invoice Number, and Final Amount for each match.
Build a Summary view that shows the owner's overall financial totals: Total Revenue (sum of all final amounts), CGST total, SGST total, and Total GST.

Provide a per-invoice PDF download/print feature. For each bill, generate a printable view (or browser-native PDF via window.print) that displays: company name 'MANISH INFRATECH', invoice number, party name, base amount, CGST 9%, SGST 9%, round-off, and final amount.

Design a professional, clean visual theme inspired by Indian business/accounting software: warm off-white backgrounds, deep navy and saffron-orange accents, clear tabular layouts for bill listings, and prominent rupee (₹) currency formatting throughout. Apply consistently across all views.

Fix the authentication/authorization gate so that when an unauthorized principal logs in, the app strictly shows the 'Access Denied' screen and does NOT render any app content, navigation, or routes behind it. The AuthGuard component must block all child rendering when the user is authenticated but not the registered owner — no routes or data should be accessible until the principal matches the stored owner principal.

Update the 'Add Bill' form to include an editable line item table with up to 10 rows. Each row must have fields for Sr. No. (auto-incremented), HSN Code (text input), Product Name (text input), and Item Amount (numeric input). GST per item (18% of item amount) must be computed and displayed automatically per row. The form must allow adding rows (up to 10) and removing rows. The overall bill GST breakdown (CGST 9%, SGST 9%, round-off, final amount) must be derived from the aggregated item totals and displayed in a live summary below the table before saving. Remove the single 'Base Amount' field and replace it with the aggregated total from the line items.

Update the invoice print view (InvoicePrintView) to include the line item table. The printed invoice must display a table with columns: Sr. No., HSN Code, Product Name, Amount (₹), GST (₹) for all items on the bill. Below the item table, continue to show the existing GST summary (Base Amount, CGST 9%, SGST 9%, Round-off, Final Amount).
Generate a small square logo image with a red and white theme displaying the initials 'MI' in bold typography, suitable for use as a company logo in the top-left corner of the invoice print view.
Update InvoicePrintView to display the company name 'MANISH INFRATECH' prominently in the invoice header and render the generated MI logo image in the top-left corner of the printed invoice.

Enforce unique invoice numbers on the backend: before saving a new bill, check whether a bill with the same invoice number already exists for the owner. If a duplicate is found, return an error result. Update the frontend AddBillForm to display a clear inline error message when the backend returns a duplicate invoice number error.

Add Edit and Delete action buttons to each bill row in the BillResultsTable (used on the Search/Bills listing page) and on the SummaryPage bill list. Clicking Edit opens a pre-filled form (reusing AddBillForm logic) allowing the owner to modify the bill and save changes. Clicking Delete prompts a confirmation dialog and then removes the bill.

Update the EditBillModal and AddBillForm on the frontend to include an 'Amount Paid (₹)' numeric input field. This field should default to 0 on new bills and be pre-filled with the existing amountPaid on edit. The pending amount (finalAmount minus amountPaid) must be computed and displayed live next to the field. The amountPaid value must be submitted to the backend on save/edit.

Create a new 'Party Summary' page (route: /party-summary) accessible from the main navigation. The page must display a table of all parties with columns: Party Name, GST Number, Total Billed (₹), Total Paid (₹), Total Pending (₹), Bill Count. Each party row must have a 'View Report' button that navigates to the individual company report. The page must include a date range filter (From date / To date inputs) that re-fetches data via getPartySummaryByDateRange when applied. Include an inline form per party row (or a small modal) to add/edit the party's GST number, which calls savePartyGstNumber. Apply the existing navy/saffron theme consistently.

Create a new 'Company Report' page (route: /company-report) that accepts a party name as a URL/query parameter. The page must display: the party name and GST number at the top; summary cards for Total Service Amount, Total Payment Received, and Total Pending; a date range filter (From / To) that re-fetches via getCompanyReport; and a table listing all bills for that party in the selected range with columns: Invoice No., Date, Base Amount (₹), Final Amount (₹), Amount Paid (₹), Pending (₹), and a Print button per row. Apply the existing navy/saffron theme.

Add a 'Profit & Loss' section to the existing SummaryPage (or as a distinct collapsible card at the bottom of the page). It must display: Total Billed, Total Received, Total Outstanding, and a visual indicator (green if outstanding is zero or negative, amber if outstanding is positive). Include a date range filter (From / To) that re-fetches the P&L data via getProfitLossSummary. All values must use ₹ formatting.

Update the BillResultsTable to add an 'Amount Paid' and 'Pending' column to each bill row, showing the respective values with ₹ formatting. These columns should be visible on the Search/Bills listing page, the SummaryPage bill list, and the Company Report page bill table.

Update AddBillForm and EditBillModal on the frontend so that each line item row contains: Sr. No. (auto-incremented, read-only), HSN Code (text input), Product Name (text input), Quantity (numeric input), Unit (dropdown or text input supporting values like SQM, RMT, PCS and free-text entry), Rate (numeric input — price per unit), and a computed Total Amount (Quantity × Rate, displayed read-only). Remove any standalone 'Item Amount' input that previously appeared in its place. The live GST summary below the table (CGST 9%, SGST 9%, round-off, finalAmount, pending amount) must continue to update in real time based on the aggregated totals from the new per-row computation.

Update InvoicePrintView to display the updated line item columns in the invoice table. The printed table must have columns: Sr. No., HSN Code, Product Name, Qty, Unit, Rate (₹), Amount (₹). Replace any previous column layout that did not include Qty, Unit, and Rate. The GST summary section below the table (Base Amount, CGST 9%, SGST 9%, Round-off, Final Amount) must remain unchanged.

Display the party's GST number on the printed invoice (InvoicePrintView). Fetch the GST number from the party profile (already stored via savePartyGstNumber) and render it in the invoice header section beneath the party name, formatted as 'GST No: <value>'. If no GST number is stored for the party, omit the line.

Add a Bill Date field to the AddBillForm and EditBillModal. The field must default to today's date (auto-populated on form open). The owner must be able to manually change the date before submitting. The selected date must be submitted to the backend as the bill's billDate (Unix timestamp in nanoseconds). On edit, the field must be pre-filled with the existing bill's billDate.

Increase the maximum number of line item rows per bill from 10 to 30 in both AddBillForm and EditBillModal. The 'Add Row' button must be disabled once 30 rows are present. The backend must accept up to 30 line items per bill.

Add a 'Download Report' button on the SummaryPage that exports the overall financial summary (all parties, all bills) as a downloadable CSV file. The CSV must include columns: Invoice No., Party Name, GST No., Bill Date, Base Amount, CGST, SGST, Round-off, Final Amount, Amount Paid, Pending Amount. Apply any active date-range filter when generating the export.

Add a 'Download Report' button on the CompanyReportPage that exports the current party's report as a downloadable CSV file. The CSV must include columns: Invoice No., Bill Date, Base Amount, Final Amount, Amount Paid, Pending. Apply any active date-range filter when generating the export.

Implement autocomplete suggestions for the Party Name input in AddBillForm and EditBillModal. As the user types, show a dropdown of previously used party names (fetched from existing bills) that match the typed prefix. Selecting a suggestion fills in the party name field. The suggestion list must be sourced from all unique party names already stored in the backend.

Implement autocomplete suggestions for the Product Name input within line item rows in AddBillForm and EditBillModal. As the user types in a Product Name cell, show a dropdown of previously used product names (from all existing line items across bills) that match the typed prefix. Selecting a suggestion fills in that product name cell.

Replace Internet Identity authentication with a simple hardcoded username/PIN login screen. The only valid credentials are username: MANISHINFRATECH and PIN: 1752. Any other credential combination must be rejected with a clear error message. On successful login, grant access to the full app. On failure, show an inline error. Store the session in localStorage so the user stays logged in on page refresh until they explicitly log out.

Revert the Add Bill form UI and the overall billing interface to match the version 6 layout exactly. Do not add any new UI elements beyond what is explicitly listed in REQ-40 and REQ-41. Remove any UI additions introduced after version 6 that are not part of the permitted additions.

In the Add Bill form and Edit Bill modal, add a Bill Date field that auto-populates with today's date when the form opens. The user must be able to manually change the date before submitting. The selected date must be submitted to the backend as the bill's billDate (Unix timestamp in nanoseconds). On edit, the field must be pre-filled with the existing bill's billDate. This is one of the only permitted additions to the version 6 layout.

Set the maximum number of line item rows per bill to 15 in both AddBillForm and EditBillModal. The 'Add Row' button must be disabled once 15 rows are present. The backend must accept up to 15 line items per bill.

Display the party's GST number on the printed invoice (InvoicePrintView) in the invoice header section beneath the party name, formatted as 'GST No: <value>'. If no GST number is stored for the party, omit the line. Do not change any other aspect of the print layout — this is the only permitted addition to the print format.

Restrict the Summary Report CSV download button on the SummaryPage so it is only accessible and functional when the user is logged in with the valid owner credentials (MANISHINFRATECH / 1752). If the session is not active, the download button must not be rendered or must be disabled. Unauthenticated or unauthorized users must not be able to trigger a report download.

Diagnose and fix the blank page rendering failure on the Billing (Add Bill) and Summary pages introduced after the version 8 deploy. Investigate likely causes including: broken imports or missing exports in AddBillPage, SummaryPage, or their child components; runtime errors from undefined variables or failed hook calls (e.g. useQueries, useSimpleAuth, useActor); TanStack Router route definition mismatches; and any TypeScript or Vite build errors that silently produce an empty render. Ensure all pages render their full content correctly after login with credentials MANISHINFRATECH / 1752.

Revert the invoice print view (InvoicePrintView) and the Add Bill / Edit Bill form visual styling to the version 5/6 red-and-white color theme. The print layout must use a red header band, white body, red accent borders on the GST summary table, and red typography for the company name 'MANISH INFRATECH'. The MI logo (red and white square with bold 'MI' initials) must appear in the top-left corner of the printed invoice header. No structural changes to the existing column layout (Sr. No., HSN Code, Product Name, Qty, Unit, Rate ₹, Amount ₹) or GST summary section are permitted — only the color theme and logo rendering are being changed.

Replace the Unit field in the line item rows in AddBillForm and EditBillModal with a plain free-text input. Remove any dropdown, datalist, or autocomplete suggestion mechanism from the Unit field. The user must be able to type any arbitrary unit value (e.g. SQM, RMT, PCS, KG, or any custom text) without being constrained to a predefined list.

Diagnose and fix the 'actor not available' error that prevents bills from being saved. Audit the frontend actor initialization in useActor.ts and useQueries.ts to ensure the backend canister actor is correctly instantiated and available before any mutation (addBill, editBill) is called. Verify that the actor IDL/candid interface matches the current backend method signatures exactly — including addBill, editBill, deleteBill, getAllBills, searchBills, and all report/summary methods. Fix any mismatch, stale import, or race condition that causes the actor to be undefined or unavailable at call time.

Ensure the frontend gracefully handles the actor initialization lifecycle: show a loading state while the actor is being set up, and display a clear user-friendly error message (e.g. 'Unable to connect to server. Please refresh and try again.') if the actor fails to initialize, rather than silently failing or showing a blank page. The AddBillForm 'Save Bill' button must be disabled and show a loading indicator while the actor is not yet available.

Restore the invoice print view (InvoicePrintView) and the Add Bill / Edit Bill form visual styling to the exact version 5/6 red-and-white color theme. The print layout must use: a red header band at the top, white body background, red accent borders on the GST summary table, and red typography for the company name 'MANISH INFRATECH'. The MI logo (red and white square with bold 'MI' initials) must appear in the top-left corner of the printed invoice header. The line item table columns must be: Sr. No., HSN Code, Product Name, Qty, Unit, Rate (₹), Amount (₹). The GST summary section (Base Amount, CGST 9%, SGST 9%, Round-off, Final Amount) must appear below the item table unchanged. No structural changes to the column layout are permitted — only the color theme and logo rendering are being restored.

Restore the login/authorization gate in AuthGuard so that the app is only accessible after successfully logging in with the hardcoded credentials (username: MANISHINFRATECH, PIN: 1752). If no valid session exists in localStorage, show the SimpleLoginScreen and block all app content. If an invalid session or no session is detected, the user must be redirected to the login screen. The logout button in the Layout header must clear the localStorage session and return the user to the login screen. No app routes, navigation, or data must be accessible without a valid authenticated session.

Fix the bill date display bug that causes dates to show as '1970'. The root cause is an incorrect timestamp unit conversion — bill dates are stored as Unix timestamps in nanoseconds on the backend, but the frontend is likely passing them directly to JavaScript's Date constructor (which expects milliseconds). Audit all date display locations (BillResultsTable, CompanyReportPage, InvoicePrintView, EditBillModal, AddBillForm, SummaryPage) and ensure that nanosecond timestamps are divided by 1,000,000 before being passed to the Date constructor. Also verify that when the user selects a date in AddBillForm and EditBillModal, the value is correctly multiplied by 1,000,000 before being submitted to the backend as a nanosecond timestamp.

Implement simple hardcoded username/PIN authentication: username MANISHINFRATECH, PIN 1752. The SimpleLoginScreen must show an inline error for invalid credentials. On success, store a session token in localStorage so the user remains logged in across page refreshes. The AuthGuard must block all app routes and content when no valid session exists — showing only the SimpleLoginScreen. The logout button in the Layout header must clear the localStorage session and return the user to the login screen immediately. No app routes, navigation, or data must be accessible without a valid authenticated session. Any browser user who knows the credentials can log in; bills are shared and stored globally (not per-user).

Implement the full Add Bill form with the following fields: Party Name (text input with autocomplete suggestions from previously saved party names), Invoice Number (unique — reject duplicates with inline error), Bill Date (date picker auto-populated with today's date, user-alterable, submitted to backend as nanosecond Unix timestamp), and a line item table with up to 15 rows. Each line item row must contain: Sr. No. (auto-incremented, read-only), HSN Code (text input), Product Name (text input with autocomplete suggestions from previously used product names), Quantity (numeric input), Unit (plain free-text input — no dropdown or suggestion), Rate (numeric input), and computed Amount (Quantity × Rate, read-only). An 'Add Row' button adds rows up to the 15-row maximum (disabled at 15 rows). Each row has a remove button. Below the table, a live GST summary must show: Base Amount (sum of all line item amounts), CGST 9%, SGST 9%, Round-off, Final Amount, Amount Paid (₹) input (default 0), and Pending Amount (Final Amount minus Amount Paid).

Implement the Search / Bills listing page where the owner can type a company name or invoice number keyword and retrieve all bills matching that keyword (substring match on partyName or invoiceNumber). Display results in BillResultsTable with columns: Invoice No., Party Name, Bill Date, Base Amount (₹), Final Amount (₹), Amount Paid (₹), Pending (₹), and action buttons: Edit (opens EditBillModal), Print (navigates to print route), Delete (confirmation dialog then removes bill).

Implement the Edit Bill modal (EditBillModal) that pre-fills all fields from the existing bill: Party Name (with autocomplete), Invoice Number, Bill Date (converted from nanoseconds to a date input value), line items table (up to 15 rows, pre-populated with existing items including quantity, unit, rate), Amount Paid, and live GST summary. On save, submit the updated bill to the backend via editBill. The modal must validate for duplicate invoice numbers if the invoice number is changed.

Implement the Tax Invoice print view (InvoicePrintView). The printed invoice must: display 'TAX INVOICE' prominently as the document title; show the MI logo (red and white square with bold 'MI' initials) in the top-left corner of the header; show 'MANISH INFRATECH' in large red typography in the header; show the party name and, if available, their GST number below the party name (formatted as 'GST No: <value>'); show invoice number and bill date (correctly converted from nanoseconds to a readable date); include a line items table with columns: Sr. No., HSN Code, Product Name, Qty, Unit, Rate (₹), Amount (₹); include a GST summary section below the table showing Base Amount, CGST 9%, SGST 9%, Round-off, and Final Amount; show the Final Amount in words (e.g. 'Rupees Twelve Thousand Five Hundred Only') on the printed/shared view only; include a 'Thank you for your business' footer. The layout must use a red header band, white body background, and red accent borders on the GST summary table. The overall design must be aesthetic and professional to attract customers.

Implement the Party Summary page (route: /party-summary) showing a table of all parties with columns: Party Name, GST Number, Total Billed (₹), Total Paid (₹), Total Pending (₹), Bill Count. Include a date range filter (From / To date inputs) that re-fetches data via getPartySummaryByDateRange. Each row must have a 'View Report' button navigating to the Company Report page for that party, and an inline or modal UI to add/edit the party's GST number via savePartyGstNumber. Apply the red/saffron theme consistently.

Implement the Company Report page (route: /company-report) accepting a party name as a URL query parameter. Display at the top: party name and GST number; summary cards for Total Service Amount, Total Payment Received, and Total Pending; a date range filter (From / To) that re-fetches via getCompanyReport; a bill table with columns: Invoice No., Date, Base Amount (₹), Final Amount (₹), Amount Paid (₹), Pending (₹), and a Print button per row; and a 'Download Report' CSV button that exports Invoice No., Bill Date, Base Amount, Final Amount, Amount Paid, Pending for the current date range. Apply the red/saffron theme consistently.

Implement the Summary / P&L page showing: overall financial totals (Total Revenue, CGST total, SGST total, Total GST); a Profit & Loss section with Total Billed, Total Received, Total Outstanding, and a color indicator (green if outstanding ≤ 0, amber if outstanding > 0); a date range filter (From / To) for all data; a list of all bills (BillResultsTable) with Amount Paid and Pending columns, Edit, Print, Delete actions; and a 'Download Report' CSV button (visible only to authenticated users) exporting columns: Invoice No., Party Name, GST No., Bill Date, Base Amount, CGST, SGST, Round-off, Final Amount, Amount Paid, Pending Amount. The download button must be hidden or disabled when no valid session is active.

Fix all bill date display bugs across the entire app. Bill dates are stored as nanosecond Unix timestamps in the backend. Every location that displays a date (BillResultsTable, CompanyReportPage, InvoicePrintView, EditBillModal, AddBillForm, SummaryPage, PartySummaryPage) must divide the nanosecond timestamp by 1,000,000 before passing it to JavaScript's Date constructor. Every location that reads a date from an input and submits it to the backend must multiply the millisecond value by 1,000,000 before sending it. Dates must display in a human-readable format (e.g. DD/MM/YYYY) everywhere.

Ensure the frontend actor initialization lifecycle is handled gracefully. While the ICP backend actor is being set up, show a loading spinner on data-dependent pages. If the actor fails to initialize, display a clear error message: 'Unable to connect to server. Please refresh and try again.' The Save Bill button in AddBillForm must be disabled and show a loading indicator while the actor is not yet available. Fix any IDL/candid interface mismatches between the frontend actor declaration and the current backend method signatures so that all canister calls succeed.

Add amount-in-words conversion to the InvoicePrintView only. Below the Final Amount in the GST summary section, display the Final Amount spelled out in words in Indian currency format (e.g. 'Rupees Twelve Thousand Five Hundred and Fifty Only'). This text must appear only in the print/share view and must not appear anywhere in the on-screen form views.

Update the main navigation (Layout.tsx) to include links to all pages: Add Bill (home/billing), Search Bills, Summary, Party Summary, and a Logout button. Apply the red/white MI branding theme consistently across the navigation header. The MI logo and 'MANISH INFRATECH' text must be visible in the header. The Logout button must clear localStorage and redirect to the login screen.

Redesign the main navigation header (Layout.tsx) with a professional aesthetic: use a deep navy blue background, display the MI logo (a bold square badge with 'MI' initials in white on a red background) on the left, followed by 'MANISH INFRATECH' in white bold typography. Navigation links (Add Bill, Search Bills, Summary, Party Summary) must be styled with white text and a red active/hover underline or highlight. The Logout button must be styled with a red background and white text. Apply this header consistently across all pages.

Apply a consistent white+blue+red color theme across every page of the app (Add Bill, Search Bills, Summary, Party Summary, Company Report). Page backgrounds must be warm off-white or light gray (not pure white). Section cards and form containers must have white backgrounds with a subtle navy blue border or shadow. Primary action buttons (Save Bill, Search, Apply Filter, Download Report) must use a deep navy blue background with white text. Secondary/outline buttons must use a white background with navy blue border and text. Table headers must use a deep navy blue background with white text. Table rows must alternate between white and a very light blue-gray. All input fields must have a navy blue focus ring.

Change all body text, form labels, table cell text, heading text, and placeholder text throughout the entire app to use black or very dark gray (#1a1a1a or equivalent) instead of white. No text in the main content area (forms, tables, cards, page bodies) should be white. White text is only acceptable on intentionally dark/colored backgrounds such as the navigation header, navy button backgrounds, or red button backgrounds.

Display all success/saved confirmation messages and success toast notifications in green text or with a green background badge. This includes the bill saved confirmation after submitting AddBillForm, the edit saved confirmation after EditBillModal submission, the GST number saved confirmation on PartySummaryPage, and any other success state currently rendered in white or another non-visible color.

Fix the line item column headers and input field placeholders in AddBillForm and EditBillModal so that each column is clearly and visibly labeled. The table header row must show the following column labels in bold dark text: 'Sr. No.', 'HSN Code', 'Product Name', 'Qty', 'Unit', 'Rate (₹)', 'Amount (₹)'. Each input field within a row must have a matching placeholder (e.g. placeholder='Qty' for the quantity input, placeholder='Unit' for the unit input, placeholder='Rate' for the rate input). The computed Amount column must display the calculated value clearly. Ensure column headers are always visible and not hidden or white-on-white.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add bill with party name, invoice number, amount; auto-calculate CGST 9%, SGST 9%, round-off, and final amount; save to database
Search bills by party name or invoice number
View total revenue and GST summary (CGST, SGST, total GST)
Download/print invoice as PDF
Single-user authentication so the app is only accessible by the owner

Create a backend data model and API to store bills with the following fields: party name (Text), invoice number (Text), base amount (Float), CGST 9% (Float), SGST 9% (Float), total GST (Float), round-off (Float), and final amount (Float). Expose create, read (all + search), and aggregate (sum of final amounts and total GST) operations.

Fix the backend authorization checks so that all protected endpoints (bill creation, bill retrieval, search, summary) return an error or reject the call when invoked by a principal that is not the registered owner. The backend must not return any data or perform any mutation for unauthorized callers.

Add multi-line item table to bills: up to 10 rows, each with Sr. No., HSN Code, Product Name, individual amount, and GST on that item. Aggregate item totals into the bill's overall GST and final amount calculations.

Extend the bill data model in the backend to include a list of line items (up to 10 rows). Each line item must store: Sr. No. (Nat), HSN Code (Text), Product Name (Text), individual item amount (Float), and GST on that item (Float, computed as 18% of item amount). Update the bill creation API to accept these line items and persist them alongside the existing bill fields. Aggregate the sum of all item amounts as the base amount and sum of all item GST values as the total GST, feeding into CGST (9%), SGST (9%), round-off, and final amount calculations.
Show 'Manish Infratech' company name prominently on the printed invoice, and add a small logo in the top-left corner with a red and white theme displaying 'MI' initials.
Prevent duplicate invoice numbers: validate that each new bill's invoice number is unique before saving; show an error if a duplicate is detected.
Add edit and delete options for bills — accessible from both the summary/revenue view and the bill listing/search results.
Add backend API methods to update (edit) and delete a bill by invoice number, accessible only by the authenticated owner.
Add party-wise individual summary page: group all bills by company/party name, show each company's total billed amount, total paid, and total pending.

Add payment tracking: each bill should have an 'amount paid' field and auto-calculate pending amount (final amount minus paid). Show paid/pending at both individual bill level and aggregated per company.
Add GST number field per company/party: allow storing a party's GST number, display it in the individual company summary and on printed invoices.
Add individual company report: for a selected party, show total service amount billed, total payment received, total pending, and a list of all related bills with dates.
Add date filtering (alterable date range) to summaries and reports so the owner can filter by custom date ranges.
Add a Profit & Loss section showing income (total billed/received) vs expenses or outstanding, summarizing overall financial health.

Extend the bill data model in the backend to add the following fields: (1) amountPaid (Float, default 0.0) — the payment received against a bill; (2) pendingAmount (Float, computed as finalAmount minus amountPaid); (3) billDate (Int, Unix timestamp in nanoseconds, recorded at creation time). Expose these fields in all bill read/search/aggregate API responses.

Extend the backend to store a per-party GST number. Add a PartyProfile data structure keyed by party name (Text) containing gstNumber (Text). Expose APIs: (1) savePartyGstNumber(partyName: Text, gstNumber: Text) — owner only, upserts the GST number for that party; (2) getPartyGstNumber(partyName: Text) — returns the stored GST number or empty string. The GST number must be included in any party-wise summary responses.

Add a backend API getPartySummary() that groups all owner bills by party name and returns an array of party summary records. Each record must include: partyName (Text), gstNumber (Text), totalBilled (Float — sum of finalAmounts), totalPaid (Float — sum of amountPaid), totalPending (Float — totalBilled minus totalPaid), and billCount (Nat). Also add getPartySummaryByDateRange(from: Int, to: Int) that applies the same grouping but only over bills whose billDate falls within the given Unix timestamp range.

Add a backend API getCompanyReport(partyName: Text, from: Int, to: Int) that returns a detailed report for a single party: totalServiceAmount (sum of all finalAmounts for that party in the date range), totalReceived (sum of amountPaid), totalPending (totalServiceAmount minus totalReceived), and bills (array of all bill records for that party within the date range, sorted by billDate descending).

Add a backend API getProfitLossSummary(from: Int, to: Int) that returns: totalBilled (sum of all finalAmounts in range), totalReceived (sum of all amountPaid in range), totalOutstanding (totalBilled minus totalReceived), and a profitLossIndicator flag (positive if totalReceived > 0). This represents the overall financial health for the owner.

Replace single 'amount' field in line items with a unitary system: each line item must have HSN Code, Product Name, Quantity (numeric), Unit (dropdown/text: SQM/RMT/PCS/etc.), Rate per unit (numeric), and auto-computed Total Amount (Quantity × Rate). Update both the bill form UI and the backend data model to store these fields. Invoice print view must display all columns including Qty, Unit, and Rate.

Update the backend LineItem data type to replace the existing single item-amount field with a unitary system. Each line item must store: srNo (Nat), hsnCode (Text), productName (Text), quantity (Float), unit (Text — e.g. SQM, RMT, PCS), rate (Float — price per unit), and totalAmount (Float — computed as quantity × rate). Update the bill creation and edit APIs (addBill, editBill) to accept line items with these new fields. Re-derive baseAmount as the sum of all line item totalAmounts. GST calculations (CGST 9%, SGST 9%, round-off, finalAmount) must continue to be computed from this aggregated baseAmount.
Display party GST number on printed invoice/bill view
Auto-populate today's date as the bill date on the Add Bill form, but allow the user to manually alter it before saving
Add downloadable report for overall/total summary (all parties, all bills) as a downloadable file (PDF or CSV)
Add downloadable company-wide report per party (company report downloadable as PDF or CSV)
Auto-suggest previously used party/company names in the party name input field when adding/editing a bill
Auto-suggest previously used product names in the product name input field within line items when adding/editing a bill
Replace Internet Identity authentication with a simple username/password login: username = MANISHINFRATECH, PIN = 1752. Only this credential pair can access the app.
Revert invoice/billing interface to version 6 layout — do not add extra UI elements; only permitted additions are: GST number in print, auto-populated but alterable date field, and row count change.
Change maximum line item rows per bill from 30 to 15 (user revised from previous request of 30).
Restrict summary report download to authenticated owner only — no unauthenticated or unauthorized user should be able to download the report.

Audit the backend main.mo actor for any compilation errors, missing method signatures, or type mismatches introduced in version 8 (e.g. changes to LineItem, Bill, or PartyProfile types, or updated API method signatures for addBill, editBill, getPartySummaryByDateRange, getCompanyReport, getProfitLossSummary) that could cause frontend actor calls to fail silently and result in blank page renders. Fix any such backend issues so all canister API calls resolve correctly.
Revert billing and invoice print format to version 5/6 style: red and white color theme with Manish Infratech MI logo displayed prominently.
Make the Unit field in line items a free-text open input (no dropdown suggestions) — user should be able to type any unit value freely.

Audit the backend main.mo actor for any compilation errors, type mismatches, or signature inconsistencies introduced in version 10 that could cause the canister to fail to deploy correctly or expose incorrect method signatures. Verify that the LineItem type (srNo, hsnCode, productName, quantity, unit, rate, totalAmount), Bill type (including billDate, amountPaid, pendingAmount, items), PartyProfile type, and all public method signatures (addBill, editBill, deleteBill, getAllBills, searchBills, getPartySummaryByDateRange, getCompanyReport, getProfitLossSummary, savePartyGstNumber, getPartyGstNumber, getUniquePartyNames, getUniqueProductNames) are correct and consistent. Fix any backend compilation errors so the canister deploys and responds to all frontend calls correctly.

Investigate and fix the missing/deleted old bills issue reported after version 10 deploy. The most likely cause is a canister reinstall (instead of upgrade) that wiped stable storage, or a data migration failure. Ensure the backend actor uses stable variables for all persistent data (bills map, partyProfiles map, owner principal/auth state) so that data survives canister upgrades. Add or verify the presence of preupgrade and postupgrade system hooks in main.mo that correctly serialize and deserialize all stable state. If the canister was previously reinstalled and data is genuinely lost from the canister, document this clearly — but ensure all future upgrades preserve data without loss.
Fix date bug: bill date showing 1970 (likely Unix timestamp conversion issue — nanoseconds vs milliseconds). Ensure date displays correctly throughout the app.
Amount in words on printed/shared invoice only (not on on-screen form view)
Printed invoice must be labeled 'TAX INVOICE' prominently
Add 'Thank you for your business' footer/watermark on printed invoice
Search by company name or bill number must return all bills of that company (not just exact match)
Bills must be stored in stable canister storage and persist permanently across logouts and canister upgrades — logging out only clears the local browser session, never bill data

Ensure all bill data is stored in stable Motoko variables in the backend canister so that bills persist permanently across canister upgrades and redeployments. Implement preupgrade and postupgrade system hooks that correctly serialize and deserialize the bills map, partyProfiles map, and owner/auth state into stable storage. Logging out must only clear the browser localStorage session — it must never affect stored bill data. Bills created by any authenticated user session must be visible to all subsequent authenticated sessions.

Update the backend Bill data model to store: partyName (Text), invoiceNumber (Text, unique per owner), billDate (Int, nanosecond Unix timestamp), amountPaid (Float, default 0.0), pendingAmount (Float, computed as finalAmount − amountPaid), and an items array of up to 15 LineItem records. Each LineItem must store: srNo (Nat), hsnCode (Text), productName (Text), quantity (Float), unit (Text), rate (Float), totalAmount (Float, quantity × rate). baseAmount is the sum of all item totalAmounts. Compute CGST (9%), SGST (9%), totalGST, roundOff (rounded integer − raw total), and finalAmount from baseAmount. Expose backend methods: addBill, editBill, deleteBill, getAllBills, searchBills (by party name or invoice number prefix/substring), getUniquePartyNames, getUniqueProductNames. All bill mutations are owner-only (validated via the hardcoded session — backend should accept calls from any principal since auth is frontend-only, but must not expose data to unauthenticated canister calls).

Add backend API methods: getPartySummaryByDateRange(from: Int, to: Int) returning party summary records (partyName, gstNumber, totalBilled, totalPaid, totalPending, billCount) filtered by billDate range; getCompanyReport(partyName: Text, from: Int, to: Int) returning totalServiceAmount, totalReceived, totalPending, and bills array sorted by billDate descending; getProfitLossSummary(from: Int, to: Int) returning totalBilled, totalReceived, totalOutstanding; savePartyGstNumber(partyName: Text, gstNumber: Text) upsert; getPartyGstNumber(partyName: Text) returning stored GST number or empty string.

Full app aesthetic redesign: MI logo (white+blue+red theme) in navigation header, all text changed to black (not white) throughout app, success/saved text in green, dark navy header with white/red accents, applied consistently across all pages (Add Bill, Search, Summary, Party Summary, Company Report)

Fix invoice line item column headers/placeholders so it's clear which field is for Quantity, Unit, and Price/Rate — ensure all column labels are visible and correctly labeled in both the Add Bill form and the printed invoice
